pr.title: refine splits and match and link itmaplib.c
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Refined item TU boundaries by splitting common-item data out of it_2725 into new it_3F14.c/.h, moving it_802759DC from ithitbox into itmaplib, and retuning itmaplib.c until it was marked Matching. The PR updated splits, symbols, build config, and broad include coverage; the bot reported +3072 matched code bytes and +8512 linked code bytes, with the PR body noting 19 remaining item TUs and 79 remaining item functions.

key_files.reason: Added the it_3F14 data/bss/sbss split and moved the ithitbox/itmaplib text boundary to 0x802759DC.
Updated data/sdata/sdata2 symbol ownership and local-label names, including it_804D6D1C size correction to 0x4.
Added it_3F14.c as Matching and changed itmaplib.c from NonMatching to Matching.
New data TU containing common item GX links, ItemLogicTable, ItCo strings, item pick tables, damage log storage, article arrays, and it_804D6D00..it_804D6D40 globals.
New shared header for moved common-item globals and typedefs formerly exposed by it_2725.h.
Removed the large common item data block and simplified some assert/rotation helper usage after the data split.
Removed declarations for the common-item globals and typedefs that moved to it_3F14.h.
Main matching work: absorbed it_802759DC and adjusted stack, inlining, constants, sqrt, angle, and control-flow details.
Lost it_802759DC because the split boundary moved that function into itmaplib.
Added an #if 0 alternate inline version of it_8027AB64_SelectKind/SpawnWeight as non-compiled matching reference material.
Many item, db, fighter, and ground TUs gained #include "it/it_3F14.h" for the moved globals.

what_changed: Added split entry melee/it/it_3F14.c owning .data 0x803F1418-0x803F1F00, .bss 0x804A0E30-0x804A0FD8, and .sbss 0x804D6D00-0x804D6D48; narrowed it_2725.c data ownership to 0x803F1F00-0x803F1F38.

Moved common item tables and globals out of it_2725.c into new it_3F14.c, including it_803F1418, it_803F14C4, it_803F1ED8, it_803F1EE4, it_803F1EF0, it_804A0E30, it_804A0E50, it_804A0E60, it_804A0E70, it_804A0F60, and sbss globals it_804D6D00 through it_804D6D40.
Created it_3F14.h and moved it_804D6D20_t, it_804D6D40_t, and the moved extern declarations there; removed the same block from it_2725.h.
Adjusted ithitbox.c .text end from 0x80275BC8 to 0x802759DC and itmaplib.c .text start from 0x80275BC8 to 0x802759DC, moving it_802759DC into itmaplib ownership.
Moved the it_802759DC prototype from ithitbox.h to itmaplib.h.

Retuned itmaplib.c with explicit padding, local temps, inlining changes, #pragma dont_inline blocks, local sqrt code, and rewritten angle/control-flow code around it_8027649C, it_802765BC, it_80276934, it_80277040, it_8027737C, and it_802775F0.
Added a static sdata2_ordering helper in itmaplib.c with volatile f32/f64 literals, matching the symbol changes that converted many sdata2 constants from address-named globals to local @NNN symbols.
Simplified it_2725.c uses of the "translate" assert string and replaced custom inline JObj rotation setters with HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z calls.
Marked it_3F14.c and itmaplib.c as Matching in configure.py.
Added #include "it/it_3F14.h" to many dependent item, fighter, stage, and db source files.
Updated symbols.txt to use local compiler-style @NNN labels for many local data/sdata/sdata2 constants and changed it_804D6D1C from size 0x1 to size 0x4.
Added an #if 0 alternate implementation in it_279C.c for it_8027AB64_SelectKind and it_8027AB64_SpawnWeight; because it is disabled, evidence for its impact is only as a matching/reference note.
smart_moves: Separated data ownership from it_2725 by introducing an address-named data TU and shared header, reducing it_2725.h from a catch-all item-global header to a smaller function header.

Moved the split boundary and prototype ownership together for it_802759DC, so the function relocation was reflected in splits.txt, ithitbox.h, itmaplib.h, and configure.py rather than only in source code.
Treated the decomp.dev broken-match rows as expected relocation evidence: the same bytes disappeared from old units but reappeared as new matches in it_3F14 and itmaplib.
Used small C-level matching edits in itmaplib.c instead of resorting to assembly: padding, local variables, reassociated expressions, inline helpers, and selective dont_inline pragmas.
Kept moved global declarations in a dedicated it_3F14.h and updated every dependent TU include, avoiding hidden dependencies on it_2725.h.
Synchronized symbol scope with compiler reality by turning local literals into @NNN local labels instead of preserving misleading global it_804D*/it_803F* names.
decomp_lessons: When a data-only item TU is split out, move both the storage definitions and the extern declarations; leaving globals in the old header makes future TU ownership harder to reason about.
A decomp progress bot may show broken matches when ranges move. Cross-check for equal-or-larger new matches in the new owner before treating them as regressions.
Small-data and small-data2 ordering can be as important as text matching; local literal labels, sizes, alignment, and deliberate ordering helpers should be reviewed with splits/symbols changes.
Function ownership changes need matching header ownership changes. Here, it_802759DC moved from ithitbox.h/c to itmaplib.h/c with the split boundary.
For CodeWarrior matching, PAD_STACK, explicit unused pads, temp variable ordering, reassociation of arithmetic, and selective #pragma dont_inline remain reusable tactics.
Prefer shared SDK helpers such as HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z when their assert strings and inlining match; custom inline copies can tie a function to old local data symbols.
Disabled #if 0 candidate code can preserve a useful matching hypothesis, but it should be described as non-compiled evidence unless the PR shows it affected output.
naming_conventions: New data split file/header used the address-derived name it_3F14.c and it_3F14.h, matching the 0x803F1418 data start.
Header guard for the new item data header is GALE01_IT_3F14.
Moved extern declarations keep address comments such as /* 3F1418 */, /* 4A0E30 */, and /* 4D6D40 */.
Common-item logic callbacks keep the existing it<Item>_LogicN_Event naming, with shared/default callbacks named it_3F14_LogicN_Event.
Global typedefs tied to sbss symbols retained address-based names, including it_804D6D20_t and it_804D6D40_t.
Local data/sdata/sdata2 constants in symbols.txt were renamed to compiler-style @NNN labels, sometimes with ...data.0 local labels, instead of fake global it_804D*/it_803F* names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted static f32 sdata2_ordering(void) with volatile f32/f64 literals such as -1.0f, 1.0f, 4503601774854144.0, 1.5f, 0.00001f, 0.5, 3.0, 0.85f, and 0.01f to stabilize literal emission/order.
In moved it_802759DC, replaced prior it_804DC73C zero-constant use with local f32 zero = 0.0f and adjusted stack padding from u8 _1[12] to a zero temp plus u8 _1[8].
Removed #pragma dont_inline around it_80276408 but added dont_inline blocks around it_80276CB8 and it_80277040, showing per-function inlining control rather than blanket pragmas.
Factored repeated collision-angle code into static inline it_8027649C_ref, including PAD_STACK(4) and a ret temp noted in the diff as permuterslop.
Reworked it_802765BC stack layout by changing pad sizes, variable order, and using six Vec3 locals passed into it_8027649C_ref.
Reassociated arithmetic in it_80276934 into multiple assignments so the compiler emitted the desired operation order.
In it_80277040, used explicit temp_f3 = 0.0f comparisons, adjusted temp_f30/temp_f31 order, and used item_gobj->user_data directly.
Flattened it_8027737C with an early return for inactive motion flags, preserving later vector math while changing branch structure.
Added sqrtf_accurate_local using __frsqrte refinement and changed return_sqrt_value to call it, replacing reliance on the removed math_ppc include.
Used explicit f32 comparisons in it_802775F0, e.g. spin_spd != 0.0f and facing/argument comparisons against 0.0f.
Changed it_2725.c assertions from an external it_803F1F00 string symbol to literal "translate" and used HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/Z directly.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR slice.

decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 68.96% (+0.08%, +3072 bytes), linked code 41.78% (+0.22%, +8512 bytes), matched data 41.20% (+0.02%, +252 bytes), and linked data 34.94% (+0.01%, +164 bytes).
Bot listed 13 new matches, including it_3F14 .data/.bss/.sbss at 100%, itmaplib it_802759DC at 100%, and itmaplib .sdata2/.data reaching 100%.

Bot also listed broken old-owner matches for ithitbox it_802759DC and it_2725 .bss/.sbss; given the split changes, these appear to be relocation artifacts rather than separately evidenced regressions.
follow_up_queries: Search future item split PRs for globals still declared in it_2725.h that should live in address-named data headers.
Audit item TUs that include it_2725.h only for common-item globals and switch them to it_3F14.h when appropriate.
Before editing itmaplib sdata2 constants, inspect sdata2_ordering and symbols.txt @NNN labels to avoid literal-order regressions.
When decomp.dev reports broken matches after split changes, compare old and new split ranges before filing a regression.
Continue tracking remaining item decomp work using the PR body baseline of 19 remaining item TUs and 79 remaining item functions.
